description: Stores per-user Daily Briefing schedule preferences. Each user has at most one row. The Daily Briefing flow (Flow 6) reads this table to determine when and whether to generate a briefing for each user.

columns.description: Display name of the user. Serves as the primary column for readability in the Dataverse UI.
Hour of day (0-23) when the briefing should be generated. Default: 7 (7 AM).
Minute of hour (0-59) when the briefing should be generated. Default: 0.
Comma-separated list of days when the briefing runs. Values: Monday,Tuesday,Wednesday,Thursday,Friday,Saturday,Sunday. Default: 'Monday,Tuesday,Wednesday,Thursday,Friday'.
IANA timezone string for schedule evaluation (e.g., 'America/New_York', 'Europe/London'). Default: user's AAD timezone.
Whether the daily briefing is active for this user. Default: true. Set to false to pause briefings without deleting the schedule.
How often the heartbeat agent runs for this user.
Whether heartbeat proactive scanning is enabled for this user. Separate from daily briefing enable.
columns._naming_note: Recommended name: cr_isheartbeatenabled (cr_is* prefix convention). Not renamed to avoid breaking existing flow references in Flow 11.

columns.description: Last time the heartbeat agent ran for this user. Used for deduplication.
Maximum proactive cards generated per heartbeat run.
Hour for end-of-day briefing (24h format). Default 5 PM.
Minute of hour (0-59) for end-of-day briefing. Default: 0.
Whether end-of-day briefing is enabled for this user.

notes.design_rationale: One row per user. The Owner field (standard Dataverse system column) links the schedule to the user. The flow runs on a frequent recurrence (every 15 minutes) and checks if any user's scheduled time has arrived, rather than relying on a per-user recurrence trigger.
notes.scheduling_approach: The flow runs frequently and compares the current time against each user's cr_schedulehour/cr_scheduleminute/cr_scheduledays/cr_timezone. This avoids the need for per-user Power Automate flows (which don't scale) while still supporting per-user schedules.
notes.default_behavior: If a user has no row in this table, the flow uses the default schedule (weekdays at 7 AM in the flow's configured timezone). This preserves backward compatibility with existing deployments.
